name: First Harrison Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank offering personal and business checking, savings, CDs, consumer loans, auto loans, mortgages, and debit Mastercards with online banking via Banking Butler platform.
description_long:
First Harrison Bank is a traditional FDIC-insured bank that provides comprehensive banking and lending services to both personal and business customers. The bank emphasizes local service and accessibility, with a website highlighting their commitment to helping customers across various financial needs. Their operations center on retail banking products combined with lending solutions, positioning them as a full-service community bank rather than a specialized lender or credit service.

The bank offers a broad product suite including personal and business checking accounts, savings products, Certificates of Deposit (CDs), auto loans, personal consumer loans, business loans, and mortgage lending. They provide debit Mastercard options for both personal and business use, with features like mobile wallet integration, bill pay, and check deposit (Cinch Deposit). Their digital banking platform, called Banking Butler, serves as the primary online banking interface and includes tools for account management and card concierge services.

First Harrison differentiates itself through emphasis on local accessibility, online convenience via Banking Butler, and competitive promotional rates (advertising 5.99% auto loan rates with APR as low as 6.18%). The bank highlights FDIC insurance protection and frames itself around the concept of being "Always Local" while maintaining digital banking convenience. Their debit cards include Mastercard benefits like ID Theft Protection, rewards programs (SimplyMiles), and various cardholder protections.

As a traditional bank, First Harrison is best suited for customers seeking comprehensive banking relationships with both deposits and lending in one institution. However, the website provides limited transparency on fee structures, interest rates across most products, specific loan terms, credit requirements, and eligibility criteria. Prospective customers would need to contact the bank directly or visit branches for detailed product information, rate quotes, and application processes. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with promotional rates
Bill pay and Cinch Deposit (mobile check deposit)
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full faith and credit of U.S. Government backing
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, CDs, auto loans, consumer loans, business loans, and mortgages
Banking Butler digital platform with bill pay, mobile check deposit (Cinch Deposit), and mobile wallet integration
Personal and Business Debit Mastercards with ID Theft Protection and rewards programs (SimplyMiles)
Competitive auto loan promotional rate advertised at 5.99% with APR as low as 6.18%
Card Concierge service for debit cardholders
Local branch presence emphasized with online banking convenience
cons: Website lacks specific fee schedules, interest rates, or APR ranges for most loan and deposit products
No transparent information on minimum deposits, credit requirements, or loan eligibility criteria
Limited detail on business loan terms, rates, or SBA lending options despite offering business products
Mortgage lending mentioned but no rates, loan types (FHA/VA), or process details provided on website
No information on customer service hours, response times, or support availability for digital banking issues
best_for: Customers seeking a single institution for both deposit accounts and consumer/auto lending
Small business owners needing integrated business checking and lending relationships
Individuals prioritizing FDIC insurance protection and local bank relationship model
Customers wanting integrated digital banking with mobile deposit and bill pay capabilities
diagnosis: First Harrison Bank is best for customers seeking a traditional full-service banking relationship combining deposits, checking, and lending products with FDIC protection and local accessibility. The main caveat is that the website provides minimal transparency on rates, fees, terms, and eligibility across most products—prospective customers must contact the bank directly for detailed quotes and specific product information.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
